title: B7. Sarcasm Classification
label: mx1_delegate_12
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B7-sarcasm-classification
Title: B7. Sarcasm Classification

Problem:
Classify three Reddit replies as sarcastic (0) or non-sarcastic (1).
Determine the 3-bit pattern.

Grading target:
Exact match on the 3-bit string.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

assessment.summary: The agent is repeatedly failing because it attempts to delegate to 3 helpers (one for each text sample) while the system enforces a hard limit of 2 helpers. This causes a runtime error. Previous attempts to address this by mentioning the limit (Iteration 8) resulted in a 'no input provided' error, likely because the prompt didn't explicitly instruct the agent to pass the input data into the helper instructions.

what_helped: Iteration 8 attempted to address the system constraint of max 2 helpers.
what_hurt: The agent's natural instinct to create one helper per item (3 items = 3 helpers) violates the system constraint (max 2).
The prompt does not explicitly force the agent to group sub-tasks when the item count exceeds the helper limit.
The prompt does not sufficiently emphasize that the input data must be explicitly passed into the helper instructions, leading to 'no input provided' errors.

recommendation.reason: The current strategy is stuck in a loop of violating the helper limit or failing to pass data. We need a prompt that explicitly mandates grouping sub-tasks into a maximum of 2 helpers and ensures the input data is explicitly included in those instructions.

history_observations: Iteration 2, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11 all failed due to requesting 3 helpers when max is 2.
Iteration 8 attempted to respect the limit but failed because the helpers were likely given empty instructions or the parent failed to pass the context.
The plateau is at 0.0 score across 12 iterations.
